exercises.title: Build prefix sum array
exercises.instructions:
Build prefix sum array for nums=[3,1,2,5,8] and answer:
A: prefix[3] = ?
B: rangeSum(1,3) = prefix[4] - prefix[1] = ?
C: rangeSum(0,4) = ?
D: rangeSum(2,2) = ? (single element)
exercises.expected_solution:
nums = [3, 1, 2, 5, 8]
prefix = [0, 3, 4, 6, 11, 19]  (prefix[i] = sum of nums[0..i-1])

A: prefix[3] = 3+1+2 = 6

B: rangeSum(1,3) = prefix[4] - prefix[1] = 11 - 3 = 8
   Verify: nums[1]+nums[2]+nums[3] = 1+2+5 = 8 ✓

C: rangeSum(0,4) = prefix[5] - prefix[0] = 19 - 0 = 19
   Verify: 3+1+2+5+8 = 19 ✓

D: rangeSum(2,2) = prefix[3] - prefix[2] = 6 - 4 = 2
   Single element: nums[2] = 2 ✓

Formula: rangeSum(l, r) = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]
Prefix array size: n+1 (index 0 to n inclusive, prefix[0]=0)

test_cases.expected:
nums = [3, 1, 2, 5, 8]
prefix = [0, 3, 4, 6, 11, 19]  (prefix[i] = sum of nums[0..i-1])

A: prefix[3] = 3+1+2 = 6

B: rangeSum(1,3) = prefix[4] - prefix[1] = 11 - 3 = 8
   Verify: nums[1]+nums[2]+nums[3] = 1+2+5 = 8 ✓

C: rangeSum(0,4) = prefix[5] - prefix[0] = 19 - 0 = 19
   Verify: 3+1+2+5+8 = 19 ✓

D: rangeSum(2,2) = prefix[3] - prefix[2] = 6 - 4 = 2
   Single element: nums[2] = 2 ✓

Formula: rangeSum(l, r) = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]
Prefix array size: n+1 (index 0 to n inclusive, prefix[0]=0)

hints: prefix[0]=0, prefix[i]=prefix[i-1]+nums[i-1]
rangeSum(l,r) = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]
prefix has n+1 elements

exercises.title: When to use prefix sums vs sliding window
exercises.instructions:
For each problem, choose prefix sums (PS), sliding window (SW), or both could work (BOTH):
A: Max sum of k consecutive elements
B: Count subarrays summing to k (may contain negatives)
C: Minimum length subarray with sum >= target (positive integers only)
D: Answer 1M range sum queries on static array
E: Subarray sum equals k (positive integers only)
exercises.expected_solution:
A: Max k consecutive sum → BOTH work
   SW: O(n) time, O(1) space — preferred
   PS: O(n) precompute + O(1) per query

B: Count subarrays sum==k (negatives possible) → PREFIX SUMS (PS)
   SW FAILS: negatives break monotonic property
   PS: prefix + HashMap, O(n) time O(n) space

C: Min length subarray sum>=target (positives only) → SW preferred
   SW: variable window, shrink when sum>=target, O(n) O(1)
   PS: binary search on prefix array, O(n log n) — worse

D: 1M range queries on static array → PREFIX SUMS (PS)
   Precompute: O(n). Each query: O(1). 1M queries: O(1M)
   SW cannot answer arbitrary range queries

E: Subarray sum==k (positives only) → BOTH work
   SW: O(n), expand/shrink around target sum
   PS: prefix + HashMap, O(n)
   BUT: PS also works with negatives — more general

test_cases.expected:
A: Max k consecutive sum → BOTH work
   SW: O(n) time, O(1) space — preferred
   PS: O(n) precompute + O(1) per query

B: Count subarrays sum==k (negatives possible) → PREFIX SUMS (PS)
   SW FAILS: negatives break monotonic property
   PS: prefix + HashMap, O(n) time O(n) space

C: Min length subarray sum>=target (positives only) → SW preferred
   SW: variable window, shrink when sum>=target, O(n) O(1)
   PS: binary search on prefix array, O(n log n) — worse

D: 1M range queries on static array → PREFIX SUMS (PS)
   Precompute: O(n). Each query: O(1). 1M queries: O(1M)
   SW cannot answer arbitrary range queries

E: Subarray sum==k (positives only) → BOTH work
   SW: O(n), expand/shrink around target sum
   PS: prefix + HashMap, O(n)
   BUT: PS also works with negatives — more general

hints: Negatives → prefix sums (not sliding window)
Range queries → always prefix sums
Sliding window: O(1) space; prefix sums: O(n) space

exercises.title: Range Sum Query — Immutable (LeetCode #303)
exercises.instructions:
Build a NumArray class that answers range sum queries in O(1) after O(n) preprocessing.
Exmaple: nums=[−2,0,3,−5,2,−1]
sumRange(0,2)=1, sumRange(2,5)=−1, sumRange(0,5)=−3
hints: prefix[0]=0, prefix[i]=prefix[i-1]+nums[i-1]
sumRange(l,r) = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]
Works with negatives

exercises.title: Subarray Sum Equals K (LeetCode #560)
exercises.instructions:
Count number of subarrays with sum equal to k. May contain negatives.
nums=[1,1,1], k=2 → 2
nums=[1,2,3], k=3 → 2
nums=[-1,1,1], k=2 → 1
hints: prefixCount.put(0,1): handles subarrays from index 0
sum-k: how much prefix we need to have seen
Works with negatives

exercises.title: Product of Array Except Self (LeetCode #238)
exercises.instructions:
Given array, return array where output[i] = product of all elements except nums[i].
No division allowed. O(n) time, O(1) extra space.

nums=[1,2,3,4] → [24,12,8,6]
nums=[-1,1,0,-3,3] → [0,0,9,0,0]
hints: Left pass: result[i] = product of nums[0..i-1]
Right pass: multiply result[i] by product of nums[i+1..n-1]
rightProduct variable tracks running right suffix

exercises.title: Find Pivot Index (LeetCode #724)
exercises.instructions:
Find leftmost index where sum of elements to the left equals sum to the right.
nums=[1,7,3,6,5,6] → 3 (left=1+7+3=11, right=5+6=11)
nums=[1,2,3] → -1
nums=[2,1,-1] → 0

Use prefix sums. O(n) time, O(1) space.
hints: rightSum = total - leftSum - nums[i] (no extra array needed)
Update leftSum AFTER checking condition
Works with negatives

exercises.title: Continuous Subarray Sum (LeetCode #523)
exercises.instructions:
Given array and k, return true if there's a subarray of length >= 2 with sum divisible by k.

nums=[23,2,4,6,7], k=6 → true ([2,4] sums to 6)
nums=[23,2,6,4,7], k=6 → true ([23,2,6,4,7] sums to 42)

Key: prefix[j] % k == prefix[i] % k → subarray[i..j] divisible by k.
hints: Same remainder → difference is divisible by k
firstSeen.put(0,-1): handles subarray starting at 0
Length >= 2: i - firstSeen[rem] >= 2

exercises.title: 2D Matrix Prefix Sums (LeetCode #304)
exercises.instructions:
Build 2D prefix sum matrix for O(1) rectangle sum queries.

matrix=[[3,0,1,4,2],[5,6,3,2,1],[1,2,0,1,5],[4,1,0,1,7],[1,0,3,0,5]]
sumRegion(2,1,4,3)=8, sumRegion(1,1,2,2)=11
hints: 2D prefix: inclusion-exclusion formula
prefix[i][j] = matrix + above + left - above-left (counted twice)
sumRegion: add 2 corners, subtract 2 edges

exercises.title: Maximum Subarray Sum (Kadane's) — prefix sum approach
exercises.instructions:
Find maximum subarray sum using prefix sums:
maxSubarray = max over all j of (prefix[j] - min prefix[i] for i < j)

Example: nums=[-2,1,-3,4,-1,2,1,-5,4] → 6

Also implement the cleaner Kadane's algorithm for comparison.
hints: Kadane: curr = max(num, curr+num) — restart when prev sum negative
Prefix: maxSum = max(sum - minPrefix) — min prefix seen so far
Both give same answer, different mental model

exercises.title: Prefix sum off-by-one errors
exercises.expected_solution:
Code A BUG: Two issues
  1. prefix[r] - prefix[l] is WRONG for l > 0:
     prefix[l] includes nums[l] itself (prefix[l] = nums[0..l])
     So prefix[r] - prefix[l] = nums[l+1..r] — misses nums[l]!
     Fix: prefix[r] - prefix[l-1] (but breaks for l=0)
  2. The n-size array needs extra care for l=0 case
     prefix[-1] would crash!
  Correct fix: use n+1 array with prefix[0]=0

Code B BUG: prefix[r+1] - prefix[l+1] is WRONG
  This gives sum of nums[l+1..r] — excludes nums[l]!
  Correct formula: prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]
  prefix[l] = sum of nums[0..l-1] (excludes nums[l])

CORRECT pattern:
  int[] prefix = new int[n+1]; // n+1 elements!
  prefix[0] = 0;
  for (int i=0;i<n;i++) prefix[i+1] = prefix[i] + nums[i];
  rangeSum(l, r) = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l];  // ALWAYS this formula

test_cases.expected:
Code A BUG: Two issues
  1. prefix[r] - prefix[l] is WRONG for l > 0:
     prefix[l] includes nums[l] itself (prefix[l] = nums[0..l])
     So prefix[r] - prefix[l] = nums[l+1..r] — misses nums[l]!
     Fix: prefix[r] - prefix[l-1] (but breaks for l=0)
  2. The n-size array needs extra care for l=0 case
     prefix[-1] would crash!
  Correct fix: use n+1 array with prefix[0]=0

Code B BUG: prefix[r+1] - prefix[l+1] is WRONG
  This gives sum of nums[l+1..r] — excludes nums[l]!
  Correct formula: prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]
  prefix[l] = sum of nums[0..l-1] (excludes nums[l])

CORRECT pattern:
  int[] prefix = new int[n+1]; // n+1 elements!
  prefix[0] = 0;
  for (int i=0;i<n;i++) prefix[i+1] = prefix[i] + nums[i];
  rangeSum(l, r) = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l];  // ALWAYS this formula

hints: n+1 array with prefix[0]=0: avoids all edge cases
rangeSum = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]: NOT prefix[l+1]
prefix[l] = sum of nums[0..l-1] — does NOT include nums[l]

exercises.title: Longest Well-Performing Interval (LeetCode #1124)
exercises.instructions:
A day is 'tiring' if hours > 8. Find the longest interval where the number of tiring days > non-tiring days.

hours=[9,9,6,0,6,6,9] → 3 (first 3 days: 2 tiring > 1 non-tiring)

Convert: tiring=+1, non-tiring=-1. Find longest subarray with positive sum.
Use prefix sums + HashMap.
hints: Convert hours to +1/-1 score array
sum > 0: entire prefix is valid, maxLen = i+1
sum <= 0: find earliest i where prefix = sum-1 → subarray from i+1 to now has sum 1

exercises.title: Binary Subarrays with Sum (LeetCode #930)
exercises.instructions:
Given binary array and goal, count subarrays with sum == goal.

nums=[1,0,1,0,1], goal=2 → 4
nums=[0,0,0,0,0], goal=0 → 15

Use prefix sums + HashMap.
hints: Identical to SubarraySum k — same algorithm
Binary array: values are 0 or 1 only
goal=0: subarrays of all 0s — n*(n+1)/2 for all-zero array

exercises.title: Minimum Operations to Make Array Sum to Zero
exercises.instructions:
Razorpay analytics: given P&L array, find minimum length subarray to zero out for balance.

Actually: Find minimum length subarray with sum divisible by n (where n = array length).
Special case: find if any prefix = some other prefix (both mod n).

Implement: minOpsToBalance(int[] pnl) using prefix sums mod n.
hints: Same remainder → subarray is divisible by n
((x%n)+n)%n: handles negative values in Java mod
firstSeen: track first occurrence of each remainder

exercises.title: K Radius Subarray Averages (LeetCode #2090)
exercises.instructions:
For each index i, compute average of nums[i-k..i+k] if the window exists, else -1.

nums=[7,4,3,9,1,8,5,2,6], k=3 → [-1,-1,-1,5,4,4,-1,-1,-1]

Use prefix sums for O(1) window sum.
hints: Prefix sum: O(1) window sum for any window [l,r]
Use long to avoid overflow (100000 × 10000 = 10^9, borderline)
Valid range: i from k to n-k-1 (window fully within array)

exercises.title: Complete M30 T3 Prefix Sum Synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete prefix sum toolkit:
1. buildPrefix(int[] nums): returns int[] prefix
2. rangeSum(int[] prefix, int l, int r): O(1)
3. subarraySumEqualsK(int[] nums, int k): O(n) HashMap
4. productExceptSelf(int[] nums): O(n) O(1) extra
5. 4 tests all pass
hints: rangeSum = prefix[r+1] - prefix[l]
subarraySumK: HashMap prefix frequency
productExceptSelf: left prefix × right suffix
